rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not invent new declarations.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or property references not present in the model.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions: numeric variables must have numeric expressions, Booleans must have Boolean expressions.
Move implementation-level bindings to the correct implementation annex scope; avoid placing implementation-level references in component type contracts.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, always validate that referenced identifiers are visible in the current scope.
AGREE expressions must preserve type integrity and avoid invalid scalar type names like 'integer' or 'boolean'.
Avoid encoding AADL property associations (e.g., Stack_Size) as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed in the model.
If a guarantee or assumption references an unresolved identifier, remove the clause rather than attempt to add architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid annex scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Helper functions and constants in AGREE must be declared within the same annex and match available AADL types.
Use only visible AADL features or defined helpers in AGREE expressions; do not reference subcomponent ports in type-level contracts.
Generated AGREE code should not introduce new package dependencies or data types not present in the input model.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by adding new declarations or modifying unrelated architecture.
Do not assume AADL property associations are automatically available as AGREE variables.
Do not mix implementation-level references in component type contracts; move valid bindings to implementation scope.
Do not convert Boolean or opaque data types into numeric expressions without explicit scalar representation.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about annex placement
Model fusion or component type annexes
